PureVu Window Cleaning is your Pure Water window washing service. We use a proprietary filtration system to remove all particles and minerals from our cleaning water. Our cleaning process can keep your windows clean for up to for up to 3 months longer than traditional window washing methods because we do not leave a sticky soap residue to attract more dirt. We include everything in our no surprise pricing, so you will not have to worry about any surprises or extra charges, even if you accurately use our online quoting and booking system and price the job yourself.

Your satisfaction is our only priority, and we want every job to be a 5-star experience for you from start to finish. If at any point in the process, you do not feel like we are delivering 5-star service, let us know and we will make it right. Even if Mother Nature decides to ruin our work and rains on your newly clean windows in the first 96 hours (4 full days), we will come and make them spotless again, no questions asked and no problem.

Some window cleaning companies charge extra to clean screens or different parts of the window, not us. We will remove and wet wash the screens, clean inside and out using our Exclusive PureVu filtered water cleaning process, clean cobwebs from around the inside and outside of the window, clean the window seals, and vacuum the sill and track to remove debris, dead bugs etc. Your windows will be cleaned using our 100% chemical free process and dry to a streak and spot free finish with no residue to attract new dirt. We guarantee you will love our service, or you do not pay. We are good for you, good for your landscaping, good for the environment.

You Chose PureVu Window Cleaning, now you can relax, sit back and just enjoy the view

When I started in this industry as an employee as time went on I started to become frustrated with and noticed a going trend amongst everybody I talked too in the industry, comfort and health issues that everyone says just simply cant be solved or chalked up to this is just how these old homes are. Such as that second floor or bonus room that wont heat or cool properly, your thermostat reading 72 degrees in the summer but it still feels incredibly clammy in the home, ice dams causing roof damage, spots of moisture damage on the ceiling below the attic, excessive dust and/or mold causing sinus problems, the home feeling drafty no matter how high you set the thermostat for heating, and the list goes on. As an HVAC professional we are supposed to be the ones to bring comfort, health, and safety to your home using our equipment (the 15-20 year investment that you purchase from us) right? At least thats whats advertised on a lot of contractors websites. Ive found that the answer to these problems isnt always as simple as installing that brand new flashy UV light or that 99% efficient furnace, but is a bit more involved. Right-sizing the equipment is critically important when it comes to delivering true comfort in your home

At Cloud PM, we understand that the market lacks cost-effective software solutions that are simple to use. Brad Artigues is the man proudly leading our team at Artigues Restoration After graduating with a BS from Charleston Southern University, in 2005, Brad experienced, firsthand, the devastating aftermath of Katrina.

CONFAC USA is a precast concrete sandwich panel manufacturer located in Apache Junction, Arizona. 

CONFAC USA hails from Denmark, born from CONFAC A/S, a company known for its specialized product range and production precision.

The concept of Confac USA came from the belief that the quality of mainstream U.S. construction could be greatly improved and its value greatly enhanced. Too often are buildings constructed as cheaply as possible with limited regard for product longevity and lifecycle costs. This problem has been compounded by the current state of the precast industry which reserves sandwich panels only for buildings of the highest quality and for those who can pay the most premium of prices.
Confac USA sees things differently. Building on our Danish tradition, we believe the superior value of high quality construction should and can be available at prices that are affordable and of quality that exceeds all others.
CONFAC USAs vision is to be the new standard of vertical concreteto be a sandwich panel specialist in an industry of generalists. Leveraging decades of experience, its unique, high quality European process and design, Confac USA seeks to deliver superior value and demonstrate the future of U.S. construction

The Specification Center allows you and your team to dive deeper into the construction requirements faster, allowing users to increase their capacities. This is done by eliminating double handling of submittals and specification review. The center is customized per project to act like a google search engine for all the products and requirements for a specific project. 

If you are an Architect, Owner, or Construction manager; you will be able to have quick insight of what the products specified on your project are. This streamlines approval process and cuts down on searching through specifications. 

If you are a construction administrator, we can provide you with quick turnaround time in product submittal review based on existing specifications. We can also anticipate submittal review workload throughout the projects timeline. 

If you are a preconstruction manager, we can help you identify all products and project requirements to estimate how much effort will go into the preconstruction phase of construction. 

If you are a contractor, we can assist in providing quick access to what has been specified on your project, the coordination requirements, and submittal requirements, from one easy to use screen. 

We can also start building out a library of specification per project to allow for cross referencing and lessons learned on each product by attaching relevant RFIs and Change orders. 

If you have already developed a risk matrix per work type we can integrate that to give you a quick overview of a project risk rating. If you do not have one we can share with you our own based on our experience and the projects requirements

organizationDescription: We spray passive fireproofing onto exposed steel beams using specialized machinery and apply paint onto any type of surface. We complete initial construction, patchwork, renovations, and additions in New Jersey and the New York City metropolitan area. The term passive fireproofing references the material used to coat the structural steel of a building to reduce heat transfer to the steel during a fire. The thickness of the fireproofing on the steel must reach an hourly rating determined by the UL Fire Resistance Directory. This rating determines how long the structure will last when exposed to high temperatures. Fireproofing is generally applied by spray or by hand. The work of the fireproofing application specialist is verified by the fire inspector to meet the building code regulations. The construction project cannot safely continue until this code is met so fireproofing is a crucially timed step in the construction process. It may be necessary to spray some sort of bonding agent or primer onto the surface beforehand to ensure proper material adhesion. It is also necessary that the material being applied will properly dry and bond within its required temperature range. We are also responsible for the surrounding area to be protected by plastic or other method so nothing will be damaged by any overspray

The Grand Rapids Chapter of CSI (Construction Specifications Institute) is a chapter within the national association dedicated to improving the documentation, management and communication of building information as used by the construction community. CSI accomplishes its mission through the development of construction standards and formats such as MasterFormat and UniFormat; the promulgation of those formats through master guide specifications and building information management (BIM) software; training and certification programs, including the Construction Documents Technology (CDT) and Certified Construction Contract Administrator (CCCA) exams; publication of Construction Specifier magazine; and an ever-expanding membership of decision-makers who identify and specify building product solutions. CSI members include a cross-section of specifiers, architects, contractors, suppliers and other construction project professionals who are touched by construction documentation

organizationDescription:
HFH of Greater Volusia County is a non-profit, Christian housing ministry that works both to eliminate poverty housing around the world and to make adequate housing a matter of conscience and action.  Habitat welcomes to the table partners from any faith -- or from no faith -- who are willing to pick up a hammer to help improve the lives of families needing decent shelter.

HFH of Greater Volusia County builds throughout the county -- Ormond Beach, Holly Hill, Daytona Beach, South Daytona, Port Orange, DeBary, Orange City, Enterprise, Osteen and Deltona in Florida.  The volunteers and business partners have helped complete 157 new homes and 15 renovations --  serving 172 families, including more than 375 children -- as of June 30, 2016. 

Ten percent of local donations are contributed to Habitat for Humanity International for overseas builds -- HFH of Greater Volusia Countys faithful tithe has built 136 additional homes throughout the world.  

Habitat is not a give-away program -- families contribute hundreds of sweat-equity hours toward their home and others.  When the home is completed, Habitat sells the home to the family at no profit and provides a zero-interest mortgage.  All mortgage payments are used to support the building of additional Habitat homes

JVM Landscape Construction is a Family Owned Landscape Company, servicing the greater Sacramento Area. The company officially started in 2013, but has roots that go back three generations. 

John Martinez  started working in the Landscape and Irrigation industry as a tradesman in the 1970s, repairing and managing irrigation systems under his father, in the agricultural fields of Selma, California. He later continued in the industry working in Commercial and Residential Landscape Construction. John started his first Landscape Construction Company in 1991 after 20 years as a full-time Landscape professional, and he continued success in that company for another 16 years. 

During the recession, John decided to make a move to residential fixtures, handling construction repair work in the Electrical and Landscaping trades. He continued success as an independent contractor, even as the work became much more competitive during the down years.

In 2013  as the economy started to improve, subsequently construction began to see an improvement in terms of projects and workload needs. In light of this, John returned to Landscape Construction Contracting full-time.

 John is now the Superintendent of Construction, and his wife, Martha Martinez, handles Scheduling and Customer Service. They are also owners. His Daughter, Lacie Martinez, is the companys Assistant Designer. His son also assists in handling accounting for the business, and is a Sacramento State Alumnus. John, his wife, and all three of his children are active in the Catholic Diocese of Sacramento, bettering their communities by serving the poor, disenfranchised, and homeless of Sacramento. 

Running full force now, JVM Landscape stays competitive by ALWAYS doing the right thing for its customers, and pricing its projects competitively, without ever sacrificing quality.

The Building Industry Association of Central South Carolina (BIA), formerly the Home Builders Association of Greater Columbia, is a non-profit professional trade association founded in 1957 for the purpose of maintaining high professional standards in the residential construction industry.  Based in Columbia, SC, it is comprised of over 800 builder and associate firms representing over 7,500 industry professionals involved in home building, remodeling, multifamily construction, property management, subcontracting, design, housing finance, building product manufacturing and other aspects of residential and light commercial construction. 

The BIA is affiliated with the Home Builders Association of South Carolina (HBA of SC) and the National Association of Home Builders (NAHB) and is ranked by size in the top 25 of nearly 800 local Home Builders Associations in the country.  During a typical year, residential construction accounts for about five cents of every dollar spent in the U.S. economy, making home building one of the largest and most influential industries in the country.

The mission of the Building Industry Association of Central SC (BIA) is to serve the association members, to enhance the environment for conducting business in the building industry and to maintain housing as a top priority in the central portion of the state.

The BIA brings together people from all phases of the building industry to represent their business interests and to promote programs, policies, and education for housing consumers to obtain safe and affordable housing

We provide quality concrete polishing services in Louisiana and Texas. Polished concrete is a durable, no-wax flooring solution that offers many benefits. Decorative concrete is a beautiful finish that offers a wide variety of colors and options, and has become a great alternative to marble, tile, granite, wood, or linoleum. Polishing is a multi-step process that allows you to choose a level of sheen from satin to gloss. Not only is it aesthetically pleasing, polished concrete is also low maintenance and offers a substantial cost savings due to its long-life expectancy.

The health benefits from polished concrete are also great. Dust mites and allergens are reduced dramatically, and it does not support mold growth. In the event of moisture related damage, simply clean off the polished concrete floor since it cannot harbor mold, mildew, or allergens. Contrary to popular belief, polished concrete floors have a high co-efficiency of friction and meet or exceed OSHA and ADA standards for slip resistance.

In addition to the aesthetic, economic, and health benefits, polished concrete is also environmentally friendly. Resources are conserved as the need for other floor coverings are eliminated. Not only does this save raw materials, but it also saves landfill space because concrete floors outlast other materials and do not need to be replaced. Energy efficiency is also an environmental plus since sunlight in the winter will help radiate heat, and shaded concrete in the summer will stay cooler and potentially lower air condition costs

organizationDescription:
The Schumacher Homes story is all about family. Its a story of passion and pride, keeping your promises and building a legacy. A story that all started with Paul Schumacher and a single house.  In the early 1990s, Paul went into the business of buying and renovating fixer-uppers. After buying, restoring and selling a few more homes, he realized there was something far more special about designing and building homes from the ground up.
 
Paul discovered that the true vision for Schumacher Homes was in designing and building fully custom homes the way his customers wanted them. Not close to what they wantedexactly what they wanted, at a price and quality level no other builder was delivering.  This personal passion drove Paul and his family to make a commitment to building a legacy of quality and affordability from one side of America to the other, one beautiful home, one family at a time.  And theyve never looked back, building an expert, passionate team that continues to grow the business by enabling families across America to build high-quality, affordable, completely customizable homes that are as unique and special as they are.
 
Today, Schumacher Homes, headquartered in Canton, Ohio, is Americas largest custom homebuilder, with 26 locations and operations in 12 states across the country. Over the years, Schumacher Homes has taken the promise of an affordable, innovative, fully custom home and made it a reality for more than 20,000 families across the country. And no two of those homes are alike.
 
Schumacher Homes has been the recipient of the National Housing Quality Award for excellence in construction standards and customer satisfaction. We are also proud to have been awarded the National Gold Winning Home of the Year and the National Gold Winning Best Digital Sales Tool/Use of Technology for our exclusive pricing system-- FrontDoor from the National Association of Home Builders

organizationDescription: A.R.T Concrete Solutions is a revolutionary patent pending polymer hybrid composite concrete repair solution. The proprietary formulation of aggregate, resin, hardener and other ingredients withstands the environmental elements of moisture and chemicals. After years of extensive research (including altitude, environment and temperature variations), A.R.T is pleased to provide this product to the market. The A.R.T formulation can repair damaged concrete in less than 30 minutes with a cure time of less than 60 minutes (Final cure time may vary depending on outside temperature and the size of the repair). It is 4 times stronger than concrete. This means less traffic disruption, business disruptions, lower risk of liability, lower labor costs and far less machinery involved. Traditional methods require the repair area to be cut, material completely removed, hauled away, compacted and replaced necessitating closure of the area. The system allows the repair to take place without significant material removal minimizing the impacted area, requiring less heavy equipment and limiting business or operation interruption

Sam had always wanted to be in the construction industry, but with the high demand of the military never had an opportunity to devote the time required. Sam was starting from nothing but had a vision in his mind. In early 2002 Sam and Sam Jr. made a trip to New Jersey, which would lead to the humble beginnings of SCI. organizationDescription:
Agape Construction began in 2009 and was founded on the core principles of customer service and integrity. With over 15 years of personal experience working in construction and a passion for excellence, I knew it was time to start my own business and have experienced phenomenal growth since day one.

As a family run business with a tightly knit group of hand-picked employees and subcontractors, I feel confident about every job I send a team member out on. Only willing to hire individuals who I know personally and who are willing to align themselves with our standards of excellence, we know that were always providing our customers with the best work possible.

As a Christian, I also believe that theres nothing more important than people. The way they are treated on a job, the service they receive, and the satisfaction they feel are all things I dont take lightly. Every project I take on is completed with love, care, and a high moral standard.

What Does Agape Mean?

Agape (a-gop-a) is a Greek word that means love. The Bible was originally written in Greek and Hebrew, so the word agape shows up a lot in the original texts. My favorite place is John 3:16, For God so loved the world, that He gave His only son, that whosoever believes in Him shall not perish but have everlasting life. The word love in that Scripture can be replaced with the word agape. In English, we only have one word for love and it can mean anything from your love of football to loving your wife. In the Greek language, there are four words for love, and agape describes an unconditional love that relies only on the giver, not the receiver. The person on the receiving end cant do anything to deserve this kind of love, nor can they stop its flow. Thats what unconditional means.

Thats why I named my business Agape Construction, because I wanted to remind myself as well as everyone I come in contact with, that I believe in this love and its power to reach and change our hearts

At DGPS we believe that the educated customer is the smartest customer which is why we have created a website to inform you about how DG Pavement Solutions can save you time and money on your next job!
D&G Sealcoating & Striping started its operations in November 2001 and became incorporated in January 2005. We knew that we would need the proper equipment to obtain larger contracts so we invested in a 700-gallon sealcoating tank. We joined network organizations, sent mailers out to prospective customers and advertised in the yellow pages.
 
As we grew, we wanted to offer our customers a sealer that was Environmentally Friendly with good availability. We partnered with Neya Industries Paveshiled  an asphalt emulsion sealer. We learned that this product did not burn the skin and did not track after being applied. Once we started using this product, we also found that it withstood heavy traffic  when applied properly  for a much longer period of time than the coal-tar sealant. In 2008, we obtained a contract for sealcoating and striping 12 miles of roads in a Bonita Springs golf course community. Once completed, our recognition in the asphalt community really soared and other contractors knew that we were someone to reckon with. Many High-End Golf Course Communities hired us for their pavement maintenance needs and led D&G to continual growth during the bad economy.
 
Our attendance at the National Paving shows also allows us to learn about new innovations that we can offer to our customers. We feel that an educated customer is a better one, and we work with our customers in scheduling the job taking their concerns into consideration. We also make them aware of the type of product we are using and why it is more beneficial. We always treat our customers with the utmost respect and address any concerns they may have while a job is going on and after it is complete

organizationDescription: Renaissance Development, Inc. is a Washington, DC-based construction company and masonry contractor. Our particular area of expertise is tuckpointing: the restoration of a historic brick buildings mortar joints using traditional methods and materials. Tuckpointing, also known as repointing, plays an essential role in the rehabilitation of centuries-old structures. In our vastly growing society today, existing hospitals are not being imploded and rebuilt, but rather they are adding additions and remodeling existing structures to abide by code and accommodate their communities. Therefore, the demand for general contractors and internal construction/maintenance personnel has increased dramatically. With that, comes more guidelines and regulations for workers to follow, and has pushed the topic of Infection Control Risk Assessment to the forefront. Infection Control Risk Assessment, better known as ICRA guidelines, is a set of standards that hospitals look to - to make sure that contractors are ensuring a safe and contained work zone while they are performing projects in hospitals. 

As a member of the American Hospital Association, ICRA Solutions mission is to be a liaison between educating contractors and hospital personnel by providing a hub for some of the top trainers in the country to gather and thus become certified/accredited in the field of hospital construction. Whether you are a General Contractor, Restoration Contractor, Mechanical Contractor, HVAC Contractor, Firestop Contractor, Cabling Contractor, Plumber, etc, and if you aspire to land more hospital projects, let ICRA Solutions be a resource for you!

As a company, we dont seek to just make a profit; we are committed to impacting our community for the better. People over profit, period.

OUR STORY: 
Bonfe Exteriors comes from a long history of successful family-owned businesses. It started in 1951 when Sam Bonfe, an Italian immigrant, began his automotive repair shop with a focus on quality service to his customers. He started with a ten-stall mechanical shop and one employee. The shop is now run by the third generation, and is recognized as one of the top automotive shops nationwide. 

Inspired by his father, Joe Senior Bonfe started a business of his own in 1998 building custom residential homes. In 2017, Joe Bonfe II, joined his father after a successful career in playing minor league baseball for the New York Mets. Together they revamped Bonfe Builders, and just a few years later, started another business, Bonfe Exteriors in 2020. 

Recently in 2022, Joe Bonfe II made the transition to partner with his wife, Kristina Bonfe to carry out their vision of a Kingdom Business. They married in 2018, and in October of 2021, the two were blessed with their daughter, Madisynn. Both are excited to raise their daughter in the business and hope she will be inspired to carry on the strong line of family-owned businesses. 

Our Company Values:
Charity, Consistent Communicaton, and Integrity

In 1978 Michael Charles started Rooter Master, setting out to provide a excellent service at a reasonable price that he could stand by until the customer was satisfied. This way of doing business has served us very well over the years. We have serviced many thousands of customers solving their plumbing problems. In the 4 decades that we have been doing business we have never had a complaint with either State Licensing Board or the Better Business Bureau.

Our focus has changed over the years. We have seen the desperate condition of the sewer infrastructure in the Bay Area. We have gone from strictly a sewer and drain cleaning company to a full service plumbing repair company, to a underground sewer repair and replacement company in the middle 90s. In the late 90s we started utilizing Trenchless technologies to cost effectively replace sewers laterals in need. Since then we have refined the process Into a very efficient option to solve your sewer problem.

Michael wanted his reputation to live on and suggested to his son Ryan Charles who has worked in the business since his early years to focus on the demand of these deteriorated laterals. As a result he started Advanced Trenchless Inc, now specializing in strictly repair and replacement.  We also have a division Sewerinspections.com focusing on our clients needs for video inspection and compliance.

With the help of his brother Jordan Bailey the sons have taken the business from a small family business into a large operation helping thousands of clients solve their sewer problems. Our mission is still the same as when we started. 
We aim to provide the best service possible, be around for years to come to standby our quality work, and the complete satisfaction of our clients

In addition to our structural repairs Premier performs fiberglass repairs to tanks, pipes and vessels, often without the need to wait for a plant outage or to shutdown the system. 
 
Our clients range from local and state governments to private corporations in the power generation, waste-to-energy, bulk storage, pulp and paper, citrus, sugar and defense industries.
 
We have been the recipient of the Award of Merritt or Award of Excellence from the International Concrete Repair Institute (ICRI) seven times.  In addition, we have had projects featured in the Concrete Contractor Magazine as well as in Realta Mapei.
 
Premier CPS currently has FDOT certification for Cathodic Protection, R&R Intermediate Bridges and R&R Minor Bridges.  We also have SBE status with several municipalities and agencies.

Please take a moment to explore our website and contact us with questions regarding our projects or any potential project you may have; as we typically offer free inspection services and budgetary quotes.
 
Thank you for your time

The Western Wall & Ceiling Contractors Association (WWCCA) is a regionally focused, local association working cooperatively with the four wall and ceiling labor groups: Carpenters (UBC), Finishers (IUPAT), Plasterers (OPCMIA), and Laborers (LIUNA).
 
WWCCA is dedicated to representing the interests and needs of signatory contractors in California, Nevada, Arizona, Utah, Colorado and New Mexico.
 
Our association is fortified by a rich history, working as a center point of the construction industry since 1901. We work cooperatively for a common goal to support our members and grow our industry.
 
Pioneer | Collaborate | Support | Cultivate
 
MEMBERSHIP
Contractor members of WWCCA receive membership to these national associations at no additional cost:
 Signatory Wall and Ceiling Contractors Association (SWACCA)
 Finishing Contractors Association International (FCA International)
 Associated Walls & Ceilings Industry (AWCI)
 
ADVOCACY
Members can rely on WWCCA for assistance and support in different areas, such as:
 Representation through contract negotiations
 Assistance with labor/management issues
 Local and regional legislation efforts
 Representation on local trust funds (health and welfare, labor/management, apprenticeship, retirement, and more.)
 First-look knowledge of industry leading changes and emerging technology
 Peer-to-peer path to company ownership
 Women in construction
 
NETWORKING
WWCCA offers a number of opportunities for contractor members to meet with other professionals in the industry including:
 Educational meetings, trainings and panel discussions
 Annual Walter F. Pruter Project of the Year Awards
 Golf tournaments
 Charity events
 
TECHNICAL SERVICES
The Technical Services Information Bureau (TSIB) is the technical department for WWCCA, providing education and technical support to the design community, code officials, and the signatory contractors for the wall and ceiling industry.
 
Contact us to learn how to become a member!

Since 2013, we have provided developers, contractors, and engineers with a foundation-forming system designed specifically for building on expansive, compressible, liquefiable, or rocky soils. Our pods - void forms set directly on the grade that when connected together, reinforced with either rebar or P/T cables, and concrete placed on top form a Biax Slab - come in several heights for different soil conditions. 

Performance
Biax pods act as a relief valve for soils to move, with the only contact area for the slab and pad being the ribs between the pods. And, less contact area means less pressure on the slab when soils move, enabling a Biax slab to deliver superior performance when compared to in-ground ribbed and/or slabs with deepened footings, as well as uniform thickness slabs.

Constructibility
Our system enables the outright elimination or significant reduction in things like import, export, overex, recompaction and trenching/digging. And when you dont have to dig in the dirt, things get easier right out of the chute. In addition, the pods can be quickly connected, with reinforcing steel placed with incredible efficiency. A Biax slab is  arguably  the easiest to install vs. any comparable alternative.

Hard dollar cost savings
Up to 85% less over-ex and recompaction. No trenching or off haul. Less concrete and steel reinforcing. Reductions in building cycletime and accompanying labor costs. And, with an unparalleled track record, decreases in future warranty reserves. A Biax foundation will not only deliver superior performance, it will do it for less cost vs. any comparable alternative.

Fault Tolerance
When engineers design a Biax slab, they do not utilize the inherent benefits of the void area in the pods because current engineering software does not have fields for inputting those factors. A Biax slab has an additional level of built-in strength thats not accounted for, leading to an uptick in fault tolerance

We joined our local Home Builders Association in 1999. We built our first Energy Star Certified home in 2000; our first barrier free home in 2002; our first LEED certified home in 2006. Since then, we have filled West Michigan with many great homes.

We are proud of what we do. Building homes is our passion and our profession. We are not perfect, but we try. Relax though, we do not ask our customers to be perfect either. We like to work together to build great homes. It is a team effort.  

We believe that you should select your builder based on philosophy. Our tagline is We build Smart Homes for Thoughtful People. Further, we promote our belief that our customers should Build with Confidence. We follow a defined process that helps us deliver on our philosophy.

In 2014, Rich served as President of the Home Builders Association for the State of Michigan.  He is also a National Director for the National Association of Home Builders.  

Heartland Builders supports our community through donations and charitable work at our churchs, local non-profit organizations and schools. We care.

Thank you for your interest and consideration. We hope you like what you see and that Heartland Builders is the right custom home builder in Michigan for you. Please call us or email us today

Kalmatron Corporation is a concrete admixture manufacturer based in South San Francisco, CA. The company was founded by Dr. Alex V. Rusinoff, the inventor of Kalmatron, in 1994 and opened its first plant in the United States in 2003. 

In addition to admixtures, the Kalmatron product line includes primers, concrete regenerative sprays, radiation shielding materials, DCM, EMS, IIW utilization technology, and protective and waterproofing coatings for concrete, wooden and masonry structures. 

The signature characteristics of Kalmatron concrete are high density (as in 100% waterproof concrete), the complete absence of shrinkage cracks, and increased resistance to environmental and chemical corrosion. 

Kalmatron is used in a great variety of applications, including road construction and repair; precast, stucco and plaster; production of panels, roof tiles and blocks; construction of parking lots, swimming pools, emergency shelter; domes, agricultural and chemical plant facilities; sewer systems and waste water treatment plants; dams; hazardous waste storage facilities; marinas, piers, and foundation slabs.

We work closely with our clients to provide the best solution for every project, whether for new construction, repair, or maintenance and preservation of existing structures. Support (including mix designs) is available daily Mon.- Sat.

Please visit us at www.kalmatron.com for more information!

Kalmatron - Inventions of the 21st Century

organizationDescription:
George Plumbing Company, Inc. was established in 1947 by George F. Saliba. Mr. Saliba began his career as a master plumber in 1920 in San Antonio, Texas. With only $200.00, a panel truck, a vacant lot on Roosevelt Avenue, and tons of courage, he started his business. Eventually, Mr. Saliba purchased a building and property on Olmos Drive. However, due to city construction, the city purchased the location from Mr. Saliba to build a drainage ditch. In 1957, with the proceeds from the sale of the property to the city, Mr. Saliba built a building and moved the business to Basse Road. Mr. Saliba passed away in 1984 leaving the company to his son George L. Saliba. In 2003 Mr. Saliba built a new building and moved the business to its present location on Spectrum Drive.

Mr. Salibas main focus was residential and light commercial plumbing repairs. Due to his high quality service through the years, Mr. Saliba built a large and dedicated customer base. Many of these customers are still with us today, and have been for over 30 years.

The reason the customers have stayed is confidence. Customer confidence has made this company what it is today. Past and present management holds customer satisfaction and confidence as the single most important philosophy in the organization.

It is our goal to make every customer as comfortable as possible, whether we are working at the customers home or business. From the first time we come in contact with a customer, we want that customer to call us for the rest of his or her life.

Although the founding Mr. Saliba has passed away, his goals and beliefs are alive and well, and continue to be the foundation of George Plumbing Company, Inc
